contest.title: 2024 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Preferably Teams)

sources.notes: Read the complete statement, input layout, and constraints: 2 <= n <= 12 and every cube value is in [0, 2 * 10^7].
Read the official contest analysis linked from Codeforces. It defines dp(Y,Z), gives the equal-popcount recurrence and base case, and estimates about 1.18e8 operations and 64 MB at n=12.
Read the Luogu contest write-up's complete C section. It independently uses x-layers plus used-y and used-z masks and gives the exact transition-count sum.
Verified the coordinate-permutation reduction, the one-to-one correspondence between feasible selections and DP paths, and the transition-count identity n^2 * binom(2n-2,n-1).

annotation.summary: Choose n cells of an n by n by n weighted cube with pairwise distinct x, y, and z coordinates, minimizing their total weight.
annotation.constraints: 2 <= n <= 12; 0 <= a[x][y][z] <= 2 * 10^7. The small n permits an O(4^n)-state dynamic program, while n^3 input size is negligible.
annotation.core_idea: Pairwise distinct coordinates among n choices force every x, every y, and every z coordinate to appear exactly once. Process x-layers in order. For masks Y and Z with |Y|=|Z|=k, let dp[Y][Z] be the minimum cost of choosing one cell from each of the first k x-layers while using exactly those y and z coordinates. From this state choose any unused y and z and add a[k+1][y][z]. Equivalently, remove one y and one z in the backward recurrence. The full/full state is the answer.
annotation.complexity: O(4^n + n^2 * binom(2n-2,n-1)) time, bounded by O(n^2 * 4^n), and O(4^n) memory; at n=12 this is about 1.18e8 state scans and transitions.
tricks: No x-mask is needed because the common popcount k determines the next x-layer.
Only mask pairs with equal popcount are reachable.
Forward transitions enumerate two unused coordinates; their exact count is sum_k binom(n,k)^2 (n-k)^2 = n^2 binom(2n-2,n-1).
Use a sufficiently large sentinel for unreachable states.

solution_variants.name: two-mask-layer-dp
solution_variants.summary: Store the minimum cost for each pair of used y/z masks of equal size, infer the processed x-layer from their popcount, and add one unused y/z pair for that layer.
solution_variants.complexity: O(4^n + n^2 * binom(2n-2,n-1)) time and O(4^n) memory.

tags.evidence: The complete optimization state is the pair of n-bit used-coordinate masks (Y,Z), and every optimal solution is obtained by the layer-by-layer mask recurrence.
Used y-planes and z-planes are encoded as bits so membership, insertion, and equal-cardinality filtering are constant-time operations.
